400.q: What is the real name of 001?
What happens to Hopper?
400.a: Thought dead, transported to Russia — rescued by Joyce and Murray in Season 4

400.q: What is the significance of Running Up That Hill in Season 4?
400.a: The song anchors Max to reality as Vecna attacks her — it reached #1 in multiple countries after the episode

400.q: What happened in the Rainbow Room?
400.a: Young Henry Creel killed all the other test children — Eleven expelled him into the Upside Down

400.q: What is the Kamchatka prison?
400.a: The Russian facility where Hopper is held — the Russians are experimenting with the Upside Down

400.q: What is the significance of the Upside Down's time freeze?
400.a: It's frozen at 1983 — the moment Eleven first opened the gate — even though years have passed

400.q: What is Max's fate at end of Season 4?
400.a: She is in a coma — Vecna almost killed her, El briefly revives her but she remains unconscious

400.q: What is Vecna's plan?
400.a: To kill four victims to create four gates and merge the Upside Down with Hawkins permanently

400.q: How does Eddie Munson die?
400.a: He sacrifices himself playing guitar to distract the Upside Down bats for the others to complete the mission

400.q: What is the demobat?
400.a: Bat-like creatures from the Upside Down — they serve the Mind Flayer and attack in swarms

400.q: What is the significance of Hawkins cracking in Season 4?
400.a: Vecna's four gates merge — Hawkins literally cracks and begins merging with the Upside Down

400.q: Who is the American in the Season 3 post-credits Russia scene?
400.a: Hopper — whose survival was teased before being confirmed in Season 4

What is the name of the laboratory where Eleven was experimented on?
How does Vecna select his victims?
600.a: He targets people with severe trauma and guilt — their pain allows him to create gates into Hawkins

600.q: What is the MKUltra connection?
600.a: Hawkins Lab draws on real CIA experiments — conducting psychic research on children during the Cold War

600.q: What is Vecna's full backstory?
600.a: Henry Creel killed his family as a child, became Brenner's test subject 001, was exiled to the Upside Down by Eleven

600.q: What is the biological nature of the hive mind?
600.a: The Mind Flayer connects all creatures through shared consciousness — they feel what it feels

600.q: What is the significance of the Nina Project?
600.a: Brenner's program to restore Eleven's memories using a sensory deprivation tank to reawaken her powers

600.q: What is the full arc of Steve Harrington?
600.a: Season 1: bully/jock. Season 2: protector. Seasons 3-4: fan-favourite dad figure who can't move on from Nancy

600.q: What is the deeper metaphor of the Upside Down?
600.a: Depression, trauma, and how darkness invades ordinary life — the shadow world represents unseen psychological threats

600.q: What is the significance of Eleven losing her powers?
600.a: She loses them at Season 3's end — in S4 she must relive traumatic memories to reawaken them
